top_categories.workout_context: Granfondo Belgium's ardennes hilly loops forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 85 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Belgium's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
80ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Granfondo Belgium, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Belgium. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 85, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
6,890ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
6,890ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Belgium, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on ardennes hilly loops. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 85 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,890ft of climbing on ardennes hilly loops does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Granfondo Belgium’s 85 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Granfondo Belgium requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on ardennes hilly loops to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 85 miles of ardennes hilly loops in Les Lacs de l'Eau d'Heure, Hainaut, Belgium.
